Browsing a statbank by keyboard

Source: vignettes/statbank.Rmd
statbank.Rmd

A statbank can be hard to navigate. The subjects sit in collapsible trees, each table has its own set of variables, and choosing the data means ticking values across several of them. It works, but it is easy to lose the thread – to forget which values you have selected in and which you have left out.

statbank_app() is my attempt at a different feel for it. It is not a finished product, just a take on what navigating a statbank could be like.

statbank_app()           # Greenland (default)
statbank_app(bank = "fo") # the Faroe Islands

You drift through it by keyboard. Tables are found in a three-column browser – parent folder, current folder, and a live preview of whatever the cursor is on – moved with h/j/k/l or the arrow keys, so you can see what a folder holds before you open it. Values are picked in popups with a search box, select/deselect-all, and a running count, so you always have an overview of what you are choosing and what you are leaving out. And the code tab always shows the statbank_get() call that reproduces your selection, so an exploration by hand turns into a script you can keep.

It is not perfect. But as a newcomer I would take this over a wall of collapsible trees, where it is much harder to keep track of what you have actually selected.

Where it could go: guidance while you drift

The piece I would add next is better guidance as you move. Right now the app shows you what values exist; the more useful thing is a hint about what they mean, right next to the checkbox.

Take a value shown in current prices or as chained values. If you want the volume development over time – real growth, with price changes stripped out – you want chained values, but a label reading Løbende priser versus Kædede værdier does not tell a newcomer that. A small note in the popup could:

Chained values – pick this for volume development (real growth over time).

Current prices – pick this for the nominal size in a given year.

So you are met with the relevant bit of guidance while you browse, instead of downloading the wrong series and only then noticing. This does not exist yet, here or in the official interface, but it is the direction the app is pointing.

Using the client directly

If you already know the table and the values, the statbank_* client is what the app builds calls to, and you can skip the app entirely:

statbank_search("national accounts")
meta <- statbank_meta("NR/NR01/NRXxxx.PX")   # the variables and values

df <- statbank_get("NR/NR01/NRXxxx.PX", tid = 2015:2024,
                   enhed = "Kædede værdier")

Selections are matched against both codes and their display texts, so you can write what you see. The full client walkthrough is in vignette("daos") under statbank_*.
